mission: Investigate whether PluginKind 'tui' is dead code or still needed for backward compatibility

entrypoint_resolution_tui_branch.purpose: Allows TUI plugins to be entry-less (theme-only)

resolvePackageEntrypoint_early_return.purpose: Only server plugins fall back to pkg.main
resolvePackageEntrypoint_early_return.verdict: ALIVE but functionally moot for TUI (TUI plugins have no entrypoint anyway)

readV1Plugin_tui_validation.purpose: Validates TUI plugin default export has tui() function

installer_target_detection.purpose: Detects TUI targets from package.json exports and theme fallback
installer_target_detection.verdict: ALIVE — actively used during plugin installation

installer_config_patching.purpose: Maps kind to config filename for config patching

installer_local_kind_type.verdict: ALIVE — installer intentionally does not handle desktop plugins

tests_exercising_alive_installer_path.description: Same narrower type for concurrency tests

smoking_guns.detail: The only PluginLoader.loadExternal call in production passes kind 'server'. These 10 lines of TUI-specific entrypoint resolution logic are never executed at runtime.
readV1Plugin is only called with kind 'server'. The TUI-specific validation branch never fires.

smoking_guns.summary: install.ts actively uses 'tui' kind for package detection and config patching
smoking_guns.detail: The installer reads exports['./tui'] from package.json, falls back to theme-based TUI targets, and maps kind to config filenames. This code is exercised by tests.

smoking_guns.summary: 9 test cases exercise dead loader paths with kind 'tui'
smoking_guns.detail: After removing dead branches in shared.ts, these tests must be updated or removed.

smoking_guns.summary: No external package or SDK references PluginKind
smoking_guns.detail: PluginKind is not exported from any SDK barrel file. No plugin registries use 'tui' kind. Zero third-party breakage risk.

recommendation.summary: PluginKind 'tui' is split: dead in runtime loader, alive in installer. The dead branches can be surgically removed without breaking anything.

minimal_safe_removal.description: if (kind === 'tui' && tui === undefined) validation in readV1Plugin

must_keep.reason: Installer actively uses 'tui' kind for package detection and config patching
'tui' must remain in PluginKind type until installer no longer references it (or type is split)
